title: Compare System Bag Out Close Out Versus System Purge On The API Page
summary: Reference aid for distinguishing System Bag Out/close out from System Purge on the Overall System Aveva API page using source-backed differences in tote exchange scope and label printing behavior.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing the Overall System Aveva API page and needing to interpret the difference between System Bag Out/close out and System Purge based on the training source.
not_for: Not for executing Bag Out, close out, or Purge actions, because the source does not provide explicit execution steps.
Not for assuming operational differences beyond tote scope and label printing behavior explicitly stated in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the API page and use the source-backed descriptions to distinguish Bag Out/close out from Purge.

safety_notes: Do not assume additional operational differences beyond those explicitly stated in the source.
Escalate if the displayed page labels or descriptions do not match the training source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Overall System Aveva API page
Source-backed descriptions of Bag Out or close out and Purge

access_required: Access to the Overall System Aveva API page
system_preconditions: Overall System Aveva API page is available for viewing
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1, segment training_video_day1_segment_0066

visual_references.description: Overall System Aveva API page showing System State, System Startup, System Bag Out, System Shutdown, and System Purge.

steps.title: Locate System Bag Out and System Purge on the API page
steps.purpose: Identify the two system functions being compared on the Overall System Aveva API page.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Overall System Aveva API page and locate the System Bag Out and System Purge items on the page.
steps.expected_result: Both System Bag Out and System Purge are visible on the API page.
steps.healthy_condition: The page shows the system-level items including System Bag Out and System Purge.
steps.failure_condition: The expected page items are not visible or the page labels do not match the training source.

actions.label: View API page items
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Overall System Aveva API page and locate the System Bag Out and System Purge items.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Left-side system items on the Overall System Aveva API page, including System Bag Out and System Purge.

stop_or_escalate_if: Displayed page labels do not match the training source.
System Bag Out or System Purge cannot be identified on the page.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The speaker says all of this is through APIs and refers to the screen as the API page; the slide shows the Overall System Aveva API page with system-level items on the left.

steps.title: Identify the Bag Out or close out description
steps.purpose: Establish the source-defined meaning of Bag Out or close out.
steps.instruction: Identify the Bag Out or close out description as the end-of-sort function that closes all chutes and exchanges totes with items.
steps.expected_result: Bag Out or close out is recognized as an end-of-sort function tied to closing chutes and exchanging totes with items.
steps.healthy_condition: The Bag Out or close out description matches the training explanation.
steps.failure_condition: The Bag Out or close out description is missing, unclear, or inconsistent with the source.

actions.label: Interpret Bag Out description
actions.exact_action: Identify the Bag Out or close out description as the end-of-sort function that closes all chutes and exchanges totes with items.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System Bag Out area and associated training description on the Overall System Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Displayed description does not match the training source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text says close out is used at the end of a sort to close all chutes and exchange all totes with items; transcript adds that close out only affects totes with items and prints labels.

steps.title: Confirm Bag Out or close out tote scope and label behavior
steps.purpose: Capture the specific Bag Out or close out distinctions stated in the source.
steps.instruction: Note from the transcript that close out only affects totes with items and prints labels.
steps.expected_result: The Bag Out or close out function is understood to apply only to totes with items and to print labels.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote scope and label-printing behavior are recorded exactly as stated in the source.
steps.failure_condition: Additional unsupported behavior is inferred or the source-backed distinction is not preserved.

actions.label: Record close out behavior
actions.exact_action: Note from the transcript that close out only affects totes with items and prints labels.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training frame and aligned description indicating close out affects totes with items and prints labels.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed or described behavior conflicts with the training source.
There is pressure to assume additional close out behavior not stated in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text says close out is used at the end of a sort to close all chutes and exchange all totes with items; transcript adds that close out only affects totes with items and prints labels.

steps.title: Identify the Purge description
steps.purpose: Establish the source-defined meaning of System Purge.
steps.instruction: Identify the Purge description as the function that exchanges all totes in the system.
steps.expected_result: Purge is recognized as the function that exchanges all totes in the system.
steps.healthy_condition: The Purge description matches the training explanation.
steps.failure_condition: The Purge description is missing, unclear, or inconsistent with the source.

actions.label: Interpret Purge description
actions.exact_action: Identify the Purge description as the function that exchanges all totes in the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: System Purge area and associated training description on the Overall System Aveva API page.

stop_or_escalate_if: Displayed description does not match the training source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript says purge is like close out but purges everything and does not print a label; slide text says 'Exchanges all totes and does not print a label at the tipper.'

steps.title: Confirm Purge label behavior
steps.purpose: Capture the source-defined label-printing distinction for Purge.
steps.instruction: Confirm from the source that Purge does not print a label at the tipper.
steps.expected_result: Purge is understood to exchange all totes without printing a label at the tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The no-label behavior for Purge is recorded exactly as stated in the source.
steps.failure_condition: Purge is interpreted as printing labels or unsupported behavior is added.

actions.label: Record purge label behavior
actions.exact_action: Confirm from the source that Purge does not print a label at the tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training frame and aligned description indicating Purge does not print a label at the tipper.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed or described behavior conflicts with the training source.
There is pressure to assume additional purge behavior not stated in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript says purge is like close out but purges everything and does not print a label; slide text says 'Exchanges all totes and does not print a label at the tipper.'

steps.title: Record the Bag Out versus Purge distinction
steps.purpose: Summarize the source-backed comparison for future interpretation of the API page.
steps.instruction: Record the distinction: close out is limited to totes with items and prints labels, while purge exchanges all totes and does not print a label.
steps.expected_result: A clear comparison is documented using only the source-backed differences.
steps.healthy_condition: The comparison states only that close out affects totes with items and prints labels, while purge exchanges all totes and does not print a label.
steps.failure_condition: The comparison includes unsupported differences or omits one of the source-backed distinctions.

actions.label: Document comparison
actions.exact_action: Record the distinction: close out is limited to totes with items and prints labels, while purge exchanges all totes and does not print a label.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall API page context showing both System Bag Out and System Purge for side-by-side interpretation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Displayed page labels or descriptions do not match the training source.
Additional operational differences are requested without source support.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide text says close out is used at the end of a sort to close all chutes and exchange all totes with items; transcript adds that close out only affects totes with items and prints labels.
Transcript says purge is like close out but purges everything and does not print a label; slide text says 'Exchanges all totes and does not print a label at the tipper.'

success_criteria: The user can correctly distinguish Bag Out/close out from Purge using the source-backed differences in tote scope and label printing.
Bag Out/close out is understood as affecting totes with items and printing labels.
Purge is understood as exchanging all totes and not printing a label at the tipper.
healthy_conditions: System Bag Out and System Purge are visible on the Overall System Aveva API page.
The page interpretation matches the training source.
Only source-supported distinctions are used in the comparison.
failure_conditions: Displayed page labels or descriptions do not match the training source.
The user assumes additional operational differences not explicitly stated in the source.
Bag Out/close out and Purge are confused on tote scope or label printing behavior.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed page labels or descriptions do not match the training source.
Escalate if clarification is needed beyond the source-supported differences of tote scope and label printing behavior.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall System Aveva API page showing System Bag Out and System Purge on the left side.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Slide text says close out is used at the end of a sort to close all chutes and exchange all totes with items; transcript adds that close out only affects totes with items and prints labels.
Transcript says purge is like close out but purges everything and does not print a label; slide text says 'Exchanges all totes and does not print a label at the tipper.'

missing_details: The source explains the difference between Bag Out/close out and Purge but does not provide explicit execution steps for running either function.
The source does not specify production stop requirements.
The source does not specify LOTO requirements.
The source does not provide a time estimate for this reference activity.
The source does not define supporting role boundaries beyond operator use.
